Hey, everyone, this is Russell Brunson. I wanna welcome you guys all to the training webinar tonight. I guess it's morning for most of you guys here. I just wanna make sure really quick you guys can hear my voice. If you can hear me, can you say yes in the chat box? So I make sure that I'm not talking to myself. Or the question box, yes, okay, awesome. Cool, well, I hope you guys are having a great, I hope I had a great Christmas in New Year's. I'm excited to be back. We actually had boys each day, had the worst snowfall we've had in five years, so no one came to the office today except for me, so I could do this webinar for you guys. I had fun times shoveling the rocks and then making snowmen with my kids, so it was a great time. But I appreciate you guys being on the webinar today, and this one and just all the webinars, so you guys know they are being recorded, so if you happen to miss one, they'll all be recorded. So I might be talking about the whole plan, as the whole as much as, say, is kind of step number one, and then next week will be part two, and part three, and part four. Most of these trainings are not gonna be super long. I would say the maximum they'll be is about an hour. So that's why today we're gonna be focusing more on the value ladder. Excuse me. And then you start thinking about how this works in your business. I don't know if any of you guys have gone into Dan Kennedy seminars, but on the wall in every one of the seminars, they have these huge signs that say, this will work in my business. And that's kind of a big joke, is that these philosophies and principles that Dan Kennedy teaches don't work in someone's business. Everyone always says that initially. But if you think about long enough, you can get these things to kind of fit into whatever business that you're wanting to do it with. Okay, it works for service businesses, it works with information businesses, supplement businesses. You just have to kind of figure out how to blend it and tweak it to make it fit for your model. So jump right in. So if you guys remember me talking about this when I was in Australia, this is something that a lot of people understand that some people don't, some people never thought about it before, but when I first thought about this, it was the huge epiphany I needed that changed my business from one that was doing six figures a year to seven figures a year very, very quickly. And I always wish that somebody would have told me this when I first got started. When I first got started online, I didn't know anything. I was pretty naive and just trying to figure out how to hustle and make some money. And what I saw people doing, I saw people would put up a website and then drive traffic. So I did that. And it worked and I started making $1,000 a month and it was the most exciting thing in the world. And then I started seeing people that they would put up like a squeeze page. And we'll talk more about these kind of things in further webinars when we start actually building out the process. But they put up a squeeze page, then behind the squeeze page, they put their sales page. So I kind of modeled that and copied that and that worked and my income started going up. I went from, I think my first month making money, I made like $1,200. My second month was $3,000. My third month was $5,000. And it just happened by watching what other people were doing and modeling them. And I started modeling this and following this, I started looking what people were doing. And what I didn't realize until like say a couple of years into it is this whole back of the, this whole value ladder thing. And when I got it, then it just, it changed everything. I literally went from six figure a business to seven figure a business almost overnight because I got that piece of it. Excuse me. In fact, part of it, and this is kind of fun for me, but part of it was when I first joined Dan Kennedy and Bill Glazer's mastermind group. And it was funny because I knew Dan Kennedy was, I didn't really know who Bill Glazer was. And somebody told me that he's mastermind groups. I was really excited. I wanted to get into it. So I called their office and found out how to get into it. And they told me the price. It was like $20,000. And I was like, wow, that is, that's like four or five months worth of everything I make to be able to go to this thing. But I really wanted to go. So I acted like very professional. I did whatever I needed to do. I send them money and let me come to this event. And my very first day, I was kind of laying out my business model for the group and I was showing them what I was doing. And at the time, I had a continuity program and I had a couple of little things. And I launched my first like back end thing. It was a $5,000 coaching program. And I showed the group that. And I still remember that I was so proud of myself and thought I had this, this perfect business. And one of the guys in the group raised his hand and said, Russell, where's your back end? And I said, well, this is my back end. I sell a $5,000 coaching program. I say, well, what do you sell the people that, that, that paid $5,000 and they get there? What do you, what do you sell? I'm like, well, nothing. That's my, that's my coaching program. That's my back end. And I said, Russell, you have a $5,000 lead sitting in front of you. Like that's the best kind of lead that you possibly want. And at first I was kind of confused. I'm like, that doesn't, like people are going to spend more than $5,000 on something. Then that night, after dinner, Bill Glaser has all come back to the room. And remember, all of us had paid $20,000 to be in the room. And we're sitting there and he pitched a $30,000 thing to all 18 people that were in the room and half of them bought it. And I was so shocked. And I said, wow, that's proof that what he's talking about works that there is a back end and there's back into the back end. And so that kind of got me really excited. So let me talk about the value ladder really quickly. And I'm gonna go over this. I know again, some of this is gonna be review for some of you guys, but it's very important. So here's the value ladder. On the left-hand axis, we have value. The more value, the better. Everybody wants value. And on the right, underneath here, we've got price. And the higher the price gets, the more excited we are. And so what's kind of interesting is as you go up this value ladder at the bottom, the value's really low and the price is really low. If I take a step up, the value's gone up and the price goes up. Take another step, it goes up and keeps going up until eventually I'm clear here at the top. Now, whenever I start creating a new business, and as some of you guys know, I have horrible ADD. Right now, we're kind of going with our tax guys right now looking at everything. We've got 12 different businesses right now in 12 different industries. Each business has like 10 different offers. But whenever we create a new business or a new industry, whatever it is, this is what we look at. Like what's the thing we really want to sell at the end of the day? And that's what I really want you guys to start thinking about. What is the ideal thing for you? First off, you're excited. You want to fulfill them. I know, what are you guys on this call? I mentioned in the email that you're selling something right now. It's a high ticket thing, but you're physically doing the fulfillment and you're tired of it. Okay? So it needs to be something that not only is ton of value for your customers, but also ton of value for you that you get excited about doing. There's some things in my business that I hate doing. There's some things I love doing. So what's the perfect ideal thing you could possibly sell that's going to be the most value to your customer and the most exciting for you? I still remember when I first, at the time we ever did this exercise, I think it was five or six years ago, it was kind of funny, it was actually Christmas time and my wife and I, at the time I had like, I don't know, maybe 10 employees and it was Christmas time and I was outside hanging up Christmas lights around my house. It was freezing cold outside and inside of my gut, I had the most sick feeling because what my wife didn't know and none of my employees knew was that the business that I was building was about to go bankrupt. I was completely out of money, I had no money left and I had to fire everyone the next morning and it was right before Christmas. I didn't have money to afford keeping everyone through Christmas. I was sick to my stomach and I was trying to figure out like, what is there? It's got to be something that I can do to magically make this whole thing be saved. And I remember hanging up lights and I was just sick to my stomach and I was thinking about thinking about everything and I was listening to interviews and I was trying to figure out something I could use to save my business. And during that night I had kind of three epiphanies. The first epiphany was I needed a really cool front-end offer, so something down here, the value ladder. The second epiphany was I need to hook that into some kind of continuity program. That's these dollar signs across the bottom are something where money is coming in every single week. And third thing is I need something up here that is expensive that people are going to pay me for. I need a high ticket type thing. And so this is kind of where this whole value ladder concept came from. So I'm sitting there at night hanging up Christmas lights and also I thought, a long time ago I did this DVD where I kind of showed the business model of my business. And I talked about all these different topics and we recorded the DVD. What if I give away that DVD for free? And people just have to pay shipping handling, I'll ship it out to them, but now I qualified a customer, right? Someone down the bottom of the value ladder. If they give value from that, they're probably going to want more. And then I said, well, I've seen all these people that have newsletters. I saw Dan Kennedy's newsletter, I saw all these other people. Why don't we start a newsletter? So we decided to kind of put together a newsletter and make that a continuity program. And then the third thing is that, look, if somebody just bought the CD and excuse me, this DVD and they learned this topic, they're probably interested in more information on that topic. What if we call them on the phone and we sell them the idea solution where they actually come to our office and we help them implement what we just taught them on the DVD. And that was kind of the epiphany I had that night. And we got so excited, I said, look, if I can launch this, I might not have to go bankrupt. I might not have to fire on my employees. I might be able to save this whole thing. So that night I call all my people up. I said, look, I got an idea. I'm really excited. Come in the office tomorrow morning. You don't know this yet, but we've got to do something before Christmas or else we're in big trouble. And so they all came into the office, we get in there in the morning and I kind of mapped out this idea. Look, this is the DVD. We're gonna make free DVD here. We're gonna do a membership site here and we're gonna sell this high ticket thing. And we got all excited and we sat down and said, okay, everyone in the office, each of you guys write an article about what you're doing in our business, okay? Because everyone was doing different tasks. We had our SEO guy, our PPC guy, and our copywriters and everyone. So they all wrote articles and we compiled that to our newsletter. So now we have the first two products. And then we needed a high ticket product. So we sat down and we said, what is it that is the most value for our customer and we'd have a lot of fun fulfilling on? And that's the same question we're gonna ask for you guys right now, because this is a very, very important part of this. I said, what is something that'll be the most value possible for our customer that we'll enjoy fulfilling on? So we got out of whiteboard and we started writing down things. And I remember it started with, well, if they came to our office, that'd be really cool. They can see what we're doing. We can actually help them. We can have our copywriter write their copy. We can do these kind of things. We started writing down all these things on the whiteboard. When they came out here, wouldn't it be cool if we could record the whole thing? So we'll get everyone a little hand recorder. Then we'll have them do this and we'll have them do this and we're gonna build this part for them. We just mapped out what would be the most ideal experience that anybody could possibly have with our company. We mapped the whole thing out. Excuse me. And that was what we kind of put out there. We said, that's what it is. So then the next day, literally the next day, we had this little CD burner. We started burning these DVDs in our office. We were making this news that we put all this stuff together and I promoted it to my existing list of customers. And that very first day, we had 800 people buy that DVD. It was amazing. They bought the DVD, it was put them on the continuity program, which I think at the time was $30 or $40 a month. So suddenly we got residual money coming in. People bought this DVD. But the residual money, it was a 30-day trial. So we didn't get any money for another 30 days. So we were freaking out because I still had no money to cover payroll. And so what we did is everyone in the office, when someone ordered a DVD, we'd call up on the phone and we'd say, look, we're doing this really cool thing in Boise. This is what it is. You'd come to our office and we'd explain exactly what it was that we thought that they would want after we built this ideal, perfect offer. And within the very first day, excuse me, the very first week, seven days, actually five business days, from Monday to Friday, we signed up 11 people at $5,000 a piece. So we made $55,000. And that more than cover payroll, that covered Christmas, got us to January and in January our rebuilds hit. And that's kind of where our business took off from there. And so that was kind of how we had built the value ladder. Front end, a continuity program, and a high ticket. Now, I don't like to be talking a lot about continuity programs during this training program at all. That's not kind of my goal. If you don't have one yet, I would definitely recommend getting one because residual income is one of the nicest things in the world. What I'm gonna be talking about a lot more is the front end and then this back end. So the front end, excuse me, the front end, if you want your hand out, that is what we call, towards the end of your hand out, so step number two is your instant result. And then step number three is your magic bolt. So what's the instant result you can give them here and what's the magic bolt you can give them back here. So I'm gonna talk about those a little later on tonight, but right now I just want to kind of just, I want to dive more into this value ladder, give you some more examples. So any questions right now about value ladder, if you guys have any questions, please answer them in the question box before I go on and start showing you some examples of some of these things. And that was pretty simple concepts. I'm sure there's not probably too much yet. Okay, so no questions yet. Okay, so I started going through this, but what you guys are doing is start, just start thinking about what is this, what could your high end thing be, your $1,000, $2,000, $5,000 thing, what could that possibly be, what would be the most value you could possibly give your clients. Start thinking about that while we're going through these examples. Okay, so I'm gonna go through some of these. So this is what I'm gonna show you guys. So I want you to show how this works in kind of the real world, right? So here's a value ladder of a dentist. I think I shared with you guys my story about when I went to the dentist, right? I didn't have insurance for like four or five years after I started my business. We finally got insurance. And so we went to a dentist and we showed up because the guy had offered his free teeth cleaning. Showed up for free teeth cleaning. While he's cleaning my teeth, he asked me if I smoke or drink coffee. I told him no and he immediately upsold me to teeth winding because I was freaking out that my teeth were yellow. Okay, then a little while later, he asked me if I used to wear braces. I told him yes. He asked him how I knew that. He said because my teeth were crooked. So then he sold me on retainers. And that's the worst stuff for me. I didn't do cosmetic dentistry, some other ones. Those are the things he signed me up for and then I signed up for the six month checkups, right? And so this is kind of the value ladder of a dentist. So I walked in for a free teeth cleaning and I walked out with like a three or $4,000 bill. Now as I said about that, no, because it was tons of values. What I was looking for, the price was good and I was happy. He was happy. Everyone came out of winter. So this is a typical value ladder for a dentist. Now some of you guys don't know already how to have a business and I hope you've looked at this since we were in Australia talking about it. You looked at what your value ladder is. When I had a chance to speak at Tony Robbins Business Mastery event, it was funny because I wouldn't talk about internet marketing. But I spent most of my time talking about the value ladder because this is really like, internet marketing all becomes really, really easy when you have a good value ladder. This is like the tip of the iceberg. This is the rest of the iceberg. This is all the other stuff under the water that makes your business successful. Everyone's got a nice website. That's the tip of the iceberg. But what makes that one website make a business $10,000 a year and then another website makes someone a $10 million a year. They can be the exact same industry. Everything else can be the same. It's all this value ladder stuff. This is what makes companies would make businesses a lot of money is understanding this piece. So again, this is the value ladder for a dentist. Now I want to show you this right here. This is actually a new value ladder that I'm building out right now. This is a value ladder that we're building out as actually using an invisible webinar, an invisible funnel. So that's why I want to show you guys this because it's really, really exciting. So I will show you. So my back end, I'm going to start. I'm going to reverse engineer this to see how I'm doing this. Again, this is an invisible funnel. So this is going to be very similar to what you guys are going to be doing on yours. So my end goal, what I really, really want is I want to find more businesses that I can partner up with where I can actually become like an equity partner. I was in Dallas yesterday with a new company that we're working with and I'm doing a bunch of work for them, but I'm an equity partner. So anything that business makes, I make a percentage up. And that's my end goal. I'll be all goal. I work with tens of thousands of business owners, but my end goal is to be partners with as many as I can where I get a percentage of their business. That's my end goal. The problem is it's a lot easier for me to take a business from good to great than it is from no business to good. And so a lot of my businesses, you're teaching people how to start businesses. And after people get businesses at a certain level, that's when I come in and say, look, you're business is doing good. I want to take it to great. This is why I want to partner with you. But I can't partner with someone who's business is just getting started because it's a lot of work, as you guys know, to get business started. You go from beginning to good, it's hard, from good to great, it's really fun. I have a lot of tricks I can do, a lot of techniques I can switch to make a business get there very, very quickly. So my whole funnel, in fact, that's my goal is to get people, where my training is getting them from wherever they're at now to a good business. And then I want to partner with them to take their good business to a great business. And I want an equity position. That's my end goal. So there's all my cards on the table. Now, the beginning is how am I going to get someone into this funnel? So I'm actually going to map this out. Well, I'll start in a second. I'll go through the process first and I'll map it out from a website standpoint. So the first thing we're launching is a free newsletter. It's a free digital copy of our $97 a month newsletter. So anyone who's ever been curious about what people pay $97 a month for, they just give us their name, the email address, and boom, we give them this free newsletter. When they get that newsletter, they're going to watch it and they're going to receive a lot of value, and then we're going to sell them a book. The book's going to be a little more expensive, not much. I think we're going to charge basically shipping handling on it, maybe a little more, but we'll be selling the book and with that book, we're going to be attaching continuity. So that puts people into my continuity funnel right here where I'm getting paid monthly for. They're getting the book and they're receiving value, hopefully, from the book. As soon as they buy the book, immediately we're upselling them into our invisible funnel webinar. The invisible funnel webinar will be $0, and then the watched webinar, after webinar, will be $97. Just like I showed you guys during the invisible funnel process. At the end of that webinar, so during this webinar, I'm basically going to teach the exact same stuff that's in the book. The problem is most people are never going to read a book because the book will take them, who knows, five days, a week, two weeks to read the whole book. So we've got a webinar and in three hours, I'll teach them all the same concepts that are in the book just in a webinar format. And then at the end of that webinar, my goal is to get people to submit their website for me and I will actually review their entire website. Now, I'm going to be charging $2,000 for that, well, probably between two and three, I'm not quite sure yet, depending if they want me to review or someone on my team to review it. And then I'll go through their website and I'll actually go through their entire value ladder because I talk about that a lot during this webinar. So to go through the value ladder, I'm going to show them all the pieces and show them what's working, what's not working, and then I'm going to give them a list. If here's all the people you need to hire to fix all these different pieces. Now, take your business from where you are, make your business great or make your business good. And after we've done the review, hopefully the goal and they'll take those things and I'm watching to see if people take action. If they go and they do these things, they implement them and the business comes back, it's looking better, then I'll follow up with them and try to get them to become a partner with me where I can actually help them take their business from good to great. So that's kind of my funnel here. I'm going to show you from a website standpoint, kind of what it looks like. I'm going to go through all these topics, you're going to learn all this stuff directly from me. So that's kind of the process right there. Do you have any questions right now about that process, about how we're doing ours? Okay. Darrell asked a good question. He said, hey Russell, right here, people normally drop off continuity programs after six to nine months. He found this to be the case. I'm asking because I have a back end product that I want to deliver by continuity and want to know how many segments I should chop it up into. That's a great question. This is more for advanced people, but I thought I'd give it to you guys because it's kind of fun. So what we're doing now is during our thing, we're selling a dollar trial and then goes $97 a month. And so then the webinar, excuse me, the sales video, I sell that really hard. And they come to the page and give them the option where they'll save $20 a month if they bypass their trial. So instead of skipping the 30 trials they start paying now, we knock off $20 a month. So now $77 a month. So that's the first step because trials are good, but if you need people to pay the full price up front, they're going to stick a lot longer. And everything is their credit cards less likely to decline. Banks right now, if you put someone on a continuity program, the first bill is like $5 and the second bill is $50 because it's different and there's no CBV2 code on the second billing. Half the banks in the world would just reject it rather gates. So we're using this because if someone's gonna build $77, the next month they get built $77 again, banks don't get red flags from that. So we want people to do this. So we give them a discount. Then they fill their credit card information in the very bottom, then we offer to let them buy it for a year. So give them a 50% discount, they buy it for a year. You could do a 25% discount whatever you want. We do upsell people to buy the year at a time because for us, if someone buys a year at a time, that's usually about double what we'd normally get someone. And so we'd rather do that for sure. So that's just kind of some more advanced stuff for people, but continuity programs aren't perfect. Unfortunately, you definitely will lose people. And things that I found that have helped people to stick the longest though is making them very step by step based. So don't give it a membership site where here's a whole bunch of stuff, but give me a site where it's like, okay, day number one, do this. Week number two, and you're pulling them through a process, that keeps people staying a lot longer. So that's just some feedback for Darryl. Okay, so let me come back to this really quick. Where we at? Okay. So this is our value ladder. And this is the main thing I want you guys doing for me. For your homework tonight, it's gonna be kind of building this out. Again, if you don't have to send this to me, but if you want to send this to me, I will review it and give you guys feedback and ideas and whatever you want. But again, this is kind of really important. What's your funnel? Okay. The bottom of the funnel can be your invisible funnel webinar, but then what's the thing you're gonna be selling up here? That's really the goal. And since you understand those two things, we can build out the rest of the process very, very quickly, but it's important to know where we're starting at and what we're trying to sell back in the back end. Okay. All right, so let me do this really quick. Okay. Hopefully you guys are able to see this. I'm gonna use a bigger marker so you can see better. Okay. So for your value ladder, again, what I'm looking for, these are the core things. Because like we're looking at this right now, the nice thing about this process is we're able to bypass, we're able to jump from like this running the value ladder up to here very, very quickly. Okay. So that question probably do, we'll do this. We're gonna make this a very short value ladder. Okay. Because with the invisible funnel, the nice thing about the invisible funnel is it takes people through a process where they get warmed up so fast and they receive so much value so quickly that most of all the stress, like all the stuff that you typically have to do by taking someone up, grading them up, grading them, all disappears. Okay. So we're gonna make our value ladder for this like this. Okay. All right. And there's only a couple pieces here that we want to focus on. So the first thing is what is your instant result IR? The back here, number two, is what is your magic bullet? Okay. These are the two core things I need back for you guys. This is the, if we can identify these in each of your businesses, it'll become now very, very simple to build out this entire visible funnel very quickly. Okay. Now I know all of you guys are in different businesses. So I'm gonna just give you guys my ideas on both of these, okay, on your instant result and also on your magic bullet. So for example, when we did Howard Berg's webinar, his instant result, he's the world's fastest reader. So his instant result is helping somebody to double their reading very, very quickly. Okay. And so that was the instant result. So come on the webinar and in three hours from now, he'll double your reading. Okay. It's completely free and at the end of it, if you like it, you'll pay $97. Okay. And our dating guy is basically they watch the webinar on a Thursday and the guarantee is go watch the webinar on a Thursday, go out Friday night or Saturday night and try to use his techniques to pick up a girl at the bar. If you don't get a girl, email us, we'll cancel your payment. If you do, do nothing, we'll be $97. The instant result, you will learn how to go get a girlfriend. Our Q-bombing girl, her funnel is basically, watch this video and I'm gonna teach you how to save $100 in X grocery trip. If you don't, then do nothing. If you do, then we'll bill you just $25, one fourth of what we save you. Okay. So again, those are all running by results. Like, what's the big promise to them getting on the webinar? Okay. And I don't know if he has no mic, Phil's name or not. He's one of my close friends in a really good marketer, but we're helping him right now build out an invisible funnel for his business. And what's interesting is he actually came out of his office last week and we're talking about this and he started giving me ideas for his webinars. He had new webinars to be like this and gave me an idea for it. And it was good. It was like a cool topic and it was kind of like people will learn stuff. But if you want people to show up in the webinar and be excited, like, you can't just be like, here's some cool stuff you're gonna learn. It's gotta be, what's the result they're gonna get? Like, what's, when all said and done, what's the thing? What's the tangible hard offer that they're gonna get? Okay. Now, when I used to do my big trading seminars, I spent a lot of time talking about the difference between hard offers and soft offers. Okay. A soft offer is something like how to knit or how to swim. Okay. Those are soft offers, like, and they're good, but they're a lot harder to sell. A hard offer is when it's tied to something. Okay. It's tied to money or it's tied to time or it's tied to pain. Okay. For example, how to sew or how to start your own sewing business. Okay. Now, that is now a hard offer, because now it's like how to start a business sewing, okay, because now you can make money with it. Or you say, how to start a business sewing in seven days. Now, you've done two things to make that a hard offer. You've added money to it and you've also added time to it. Okay. How to learn how to read. Okay. How to read is a very soft topic. How to double your reading is a very hard topic. How to double your reading in four hours is a very, very hard topic. Okay. Every time I can make it more specific and more of a tangible result, the more hard of an offer it is. Okay. So, we want a hard offer, a hard, irresistible offer, if that makes sense. Okay. I'm trying to think about some of your guys' businesses. So, some of your businesses I know about, someone's got an energy business. Okay. So, what could be the immediate result? So, if I'm working with a client, what kind of immediate result is a client going to want? Okay. Maybe it's learn how to knock your energy bill in half in the next 90 minutes. Okay. Now, if they're paying 500 bucks a month and you drop in half, they just say 250 bucks a month, someone pay 97 bucks to hear that. Yeah, they will. Right. Okay. That's a hard offer. Someone else is a life coach. Okay. So, life coach, what is an instant result you can give somebody? Okay. I know when I went to Tony Robbins event, I had a lot of breakthroughs. Again, these results don't have to be huge. They just have to be something that somebody wants and a desire. Okay. More self-esteem. Okay. More self-esteem in five minutes. More self-esteem in 90 minutes. Whatever it is. You know, again, if I sat down here, these are your businesses, I could kind of brainstorm and try to think of one. But that's kind of, you know, on your side, what is that thing that you can give people? Okay. Now, one thing that's kind of interesting to think about between the instant result and the magic bullet, a lot of people are nervous about. Okay. A lot of people say, well, my instant result, I'm going to teach them my whole process. And my magic bullet now is like me doing it for them. Like, why would somebody ever want the magic bullet? Okay. And this is what I want to tell you guys. Like what I found is you can give people everything down here and you should. Like the more value you give here, the more they're going to want you to do it for them. Okay. Right in my affiliate boot camp, invisible funnel, I was on for four hours. I went to every single site I used. I bought the website. I showed them where I bought it. I went and bought a logo. I showed them how I did it. I showed them where I did every single step in the process. So simple that anybody could watch that video and just copy it and just follow it. And then at the end of it, my magic bullet, all it was was, hey, all the stuff I just showed you, I'll do 40 for five grand, who wants in, and suddenly the order started flooding in. Okay. So don't be scared of showing everything. Okay. With Howard Berg, he taught people how to double their reading. That's his big hook. Okay. He gave them everything he had. And then back in, he was selling more of an advanced learning program. Okay. But people got that result. They learned a double the reading like, wow, what else can I get from this guy? Okay. And because of that, boom, they came up for the magic bullet. Okay. So don't be scared of giving away all your best stuff. Don't be scared of giving them, you know, teach them everything you might teach in a different thing, because they're still gonna want to send, they receive value here, they're gonna want the next thing. Okay. It still kind of makes me laugh. I remember when we had our coaching program, the call center, we would, people pay $5,000 to be in the coaching program. And our coaches would teach people exactly how to build the website. And they'd say, here's the designer to hire. This guy's awesome. This is the rates, just go hire him. Here's the place to do this. And they'd give people everything and people would never do it. And then a while later, I remember like a year into, we offered a program where for an extra like $2,000, we would just go hire those people for them. And it was like 50% of the people instantly took that. Because all they were doing is sending out the exact same emails we told them to send, but they didn't want to do it. They wanted us to do it for them. Okay. That's what you understand. Your instant result, you're giving them something like that, but give them as much as you can. Give as much value as possible. And I promise you, if you do that, people are gonna want to send up to your magic bullet. Okay. So instant results, I want to talk, I also want to ask you guys, if CVS have questions about that before we move on, because this piece of it is so important. And this is where some of you guys have kind of been stuck on, is like, what is my, what's my instant result? What kind of result can I actually give somebody my value ladder? So let me know if you guys have any questions or if you want to throw me out one of yours and I can kind of dig a little deeper into that. I can, we can do one or two on the fly right now. If you guys want, if not, we can kind of move on. All right. Looks like no one has a question yet. So, okay, we'll just keep moving on unless something jumps out really quick. Okay. So again, this is your homework, guys. Basically, I want each of you guys to do within the next two or three days is emailing me that saying, my instant result is this, my magic bullet is this. Okay. So if you respond back to any emails I've sent you about this training, those come directly back to my inbox. Good question. Okay. All right. Now, I'm gonna detour outside the outline because I should have put this in outline, but I want to talk about now because it's so, so important. Okay. In fact, I wonder if, let me open a new, okay, if I do, I'll just do it away. Okay. So your instant results. So I want to talk about really quick how I built my Dotcom secrets business. I think it's interesting. I think it might help some of you guys. So when I first got started in this business, I wasn't an internet marketing teacher. I was doing stuff. I was creating software. I was creating DVDs on potato guns and all sorts of fun stuff, right? I was having fun selling all these things and had a great time. And one of my friends was running an internet marketing seminar and he said, Russell, I'd love for you to come and speak at my seminar. And so I was kind of honored and excited to come and just show people what I was doing. So I told him, yes. And then I emailed my list telling him, I'm going to be speaking this webinar. If you want to learn what I'm doing, please come to this, to this event. And then I told him, I said, look, if, you know, at these events, there'll be a lot of speakers talking about a lot of stuff, you're going to get confused. So I'm going to do like a pretty webinar the night before, or pretty seminar the night before you can come to, if you register for the events or my affiliate link. And so what happened? I got like 15 people to sign up for this event through my link. So I go to the event and I'd never spoken in public before ever. I was scared to death. I show up at the event and I set up my little room where I was going to do this event. And I thought there were gonna be 15 people coming to it, right? But as well, you guys know, when you go to an event the night before using everyone's no tell room and they're having fun together. And some guys come to my event told somebody else and they told somebody else. And so when I walked in the room at like six o'clock at night to start my event, I walked in and the room was packed. They're like 80 something people in this room. And again, this is, you know, Russell Brunson. I think I was 22 or 23 time. I was scared to death. I never talked to anybody before. I never talked about my business before. And so I'm freaking out and I walk in there and I see 80 people. I said, Hey guys, I left my pad of paper upstairs. I'll be right back. And I jumped in the elevator, went with my hotel room and I got my paper out. I sat there panicking thinking, what in the world am I going to teach these people for the next two hours? Like I was planning to do Q&As. Now I got a group of people waiting to learn from me. So I looked out the pad of paper and I started saying, Okay, if I was going to be teaching my best friend how to do this business, what would I do? What would step number one do? And I built that I said, step number one is this. I said, step number two is this. Step number three is this. Step number four is this. Step number five is this. And I think I like nine or 10 steps, right? So I got those steps and this became my, oops, let's see, it's became my system. Okay. So I walked down to the seminar room, I put the pad of paper down, I said, Welcome to my seminar. And I started teaching and I taught step number one first, I whiteboarded out for people, I showed them what I do. And then I asked questions, I went to step number two, taught that, I said three, set four, set five, set six, and after two hours, I completed this whole thing. I ended the event, I ran my room and I hid for the rest of the night because I was so scared. But that's how I taught my very first workshop. Now fast forward now to a year later, this is when my business is kind of collapsing around me. And I'm thinking about like, what do I need? What do I need? Or I need a front end product. I took the DVD for this event. This DVD I covered these nine or 10 points. So I took that DVD I took. So this is my system, took my system, I put it and we burned this DVD under DVD, and that became the free DVD that we sold. Now when we published our newsletter, all we did is each month we took one of each of these topics, we had some right article each one of these things. And so they got the newsletter each month, boom, it was like here's the nine or 10 things that reinforced what we taught back here. This is like the system, right? We taught them, we taught like, what's the new stuff that's working? Then we call these people on the phone. So look, you learn these 10 steps, how do you come to a workshop? And the workshop, Russell's actually going to teach you in person these 10 steps. And that's people paid $5,000 for. What's the same thing? If I look at almost every product I've launched in my business since then, it's a variation of one of these steps. So for example, I might go and create a product where I take just this topic right here, say this topic's copyrighting. I'll pull it out and I can create a whole product now just on copyrighting. I take this part, I can pull it out and I can make a whole product on this as well. Each of these topics could by themselves be a standalone thing, right? Now if you think about it, this DVD was like two hours long, right? This event was three days long. Now what I taught in the DVD, what I taught the event was pretty much identical, right? The same content. Yet people over here were getting it for free, they're paying shipping and handling. Let me see. Over here was free and over here it was $5,000. So what's the difference here? That's something I really want, that's really important. I want to make sure you guys understand is that people will pay more money for the exact same content packaged in a different way. You can say that again. People will pay more money for the exact same content packaged in different ways. For example, when I first got in this business, everybody was talking about the book, Think and Grow Rich from Napoleon Hill. And they're talking about Napoleon Hill and about you need to read this book, you need to read this book. So finally I went to Barnes and Noble and I paid $10 and I bought a copy of that book. And that book sat on my bookshelf for probably two years, I never read it. And finally one day I felt so dumb when someone asked me a question about it. I said, I've never actually read it. I've owned the book for two years, I've never read it. And I jumped on eBay that night and I found somebody was selling Think and Grow Rich on audiobook for $97. So I jumped online, I bought it, I got shipped to me and over the next two weeks I listened to Think and Grow Rich in my car every single day when I was driving. Now I want to ask you, was there any difference between Think and Grow Rich the book and Think and Grow Rich the CD series? No, they were exactly the same word for word, the guy just read the book. Yet the book cost me $10 and the CD series cost me $100, $10 is as much money for the exact same content packaged in a different way. And when I realized that, that's what had my big epiphany is that the different ways that you teach people and show people stuff, you can charge a lot more money for. For example, this is the DVD, even two hours I cover kind of overview, here's everything I do. But if people wanted to dig deep down and really find out all the secrets behind what we do and go in a lot more depth and a lot more detail, then they got to pay more money. They come to an event, they get to ask me questions, they get to know what other people, the experience changes things. The experience now makes us worth $5,000. And so that's kind of the process. So for a lot of you guys, some of you guys who have like, I think for any business, you need to have some kind of system. What's the system that you're going to be able to teach people? Because if you're on a webinar, you got to teach them something, teach them how to save money on electricity, you're teaching them how to be happy in their life, you're teaching them how to speed read your teaching, whatever is that everyone of you guys need to have some kind of system. And the way I develop all my systems and every inch I go through nowadays is that basically I create a table of contents. If you look at any book at the bookstore, and you open up the very beginning, there's a table of contents, and that table of contents teaches you, it shows you what they're going to be covering throughout the book. So I would go and I create a table of contents that's my system. And the system becomes the beginning of everything. Now again, this is kind of the path that we launched with my initial DocCom secret stuff, but this is the same thing. This free thing that we're doing right now is no longer DVD. What we're doing instead, sorry, the racer is not very fast on this. So what we're doing instead is we are doing a webinar. It's an invisible funnel webinar where it's $0 up front. And then boom, we build them whatever. Everyone's markets can be different. Let's just say $97, right? So this is the front of things. If I was doing this right now today, I would be selling this, and this would be my system. I'm gonna teach you guys this system in two hours, or three hours, or four hours. I'm gonna give them everything I got as much the values I can possibly give them. And the end event, I'm saying, look, if you want more, if you want to send up the value ladder, come hang out with me. And again, your back end thing can be anything. I remember the way that I came up with this whole, come hang out with Russell on my office thing, is I had a... Some of you guys know who Matt Furie is, but I joined Matt Furie's mastermind group. And I think that one was like $12,000 or $15,000 a year, something like that. I'm in the mastermind group, and I get on the very first trading call with him, and he's talking to some guys, and the first guy he's talking to is this weightlifting guy. And the guy's like, I'm trying to figure out how to make more money in my business, and it's so funny because Matt said, you know what I would do if I was you? If I was you, I would put on a workshop at your house for $5,000 and tell your entire list that for $5,000, then come to your gym and work out with you at your house. And the guy was like, that's a great idea. I'm gonna go do it. And the next person got on, and the guy was like, I don't know, a writer or something like that, and his list, he taught people how to write. He gets on there and basically said, hey, what you should do is you should put on an event where people come to your house and they write with you for $5,000. And the guy was like, that's a good idea. Anyway, you do that like four or five times in a row, and I'm sitting there listening to this, I said, you know what? I should do this exact thing. I should invite people to come to my house, or come to my office, and I'm gonna teach them how to do that. Now, it's like the very first way that I ever had my very first backend program was kind of like that thought process. So for you guys, what's the same thing? Again, this comes back to what we talked about earlier with this whole magic bullet thing, what is that thing that people want? Is it time with you? If you're doing one-on-one stuff right now, it's very easy to start shifting this from a one-on-one thing to like a group session. We have our, one of my businesses is we help people overcome pornography addictions. And so we have a counselor, and it's funny because his day job, he works one-on-one with people. He makes like $150 an hour, so he's doing great, but he's tired. He spends like eight hours a day hearing people talk about their problems, right? It's really depressing for him. So we're shifting this whole thing now with him. We're basically, we're selling information online, and now we're doing group sessions where people come like once a month, and they're paying like $1,000 at a time. They'll get like 10, 20 of them in a room at a time. He works with them like that. Okay? And what happens is he does it training once, and he'll make $20,000 where as opposed to him making $20,000 for one-on-one sessions, he's a long, long, long time. Okay? So again, what's this ideal thing back here? What does it look like? Some of you guys who are in the service businesses like our electricity guy, your business, you're going out and you're actually, I believe, I don't know, maybe made wrong, but you're going into, you're actually going through the house and doing this whole process for them, right? So here you're training. Here's how to save $250 a month on your energy. Then boom up here. I'll come to your house and just do this whole thing for you. Okay? People are going to jump out like crazy. You know, if life coach here, you teach this concept, then boom here, we're doing group sessions. Like right now, we're like, if you guys look at this right now, I'm doing a group session. We have, I think about 15 of you guys live online. I think we got another 15 who are on right now. So I'm fulfilling this for 30 people right now all at once, and it's really fun. And when it's done, I'm going to record this and I can use this in the future to kind of take myself out of it. So how do I switch from a one-on-one model to me teaching a whole bunch of people at once? It's very simple. It's very easy just by using technology. And the nice thing is that people value this kind of stuff a lot more as well. Again, one-on-one for our pornography addiction guy, he's getting $100 to $200 per session, but people pay him $1,000 to come on a group session with a whole bunch of people where they get more value, they get more stuff, and just a better thing.
